message:
chore(beads): add follow-on tracking entries for sse fuzz and review-a9 sweep

Adds two new beads issue entries that surfaced during the latest review pass:

- flywheel_connectors-3hrw3 — companion to the SSE event-stream fuzz target
  (commit d25c9216 above), captured so coverage gaps and any crashes from
  the harness have a place to land.
- flywheel_connectors-xirgb — additional REVIEW-A9 follow-on item not yet
  paired with a code commit; tracked so the audit pass has a complete
  ledger of both closed and outstanding items.

Pure tracker-state addition (2 lines appended to .beads/issues.jsonl);
no source changes accompany this commit by design — the fuzz-target work
already shipped in d25c9216 and the second entry is open work for a
future pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3gzz","title":"[SC] Tests: unit + integration","description":"# [SC] Tests: unit + integration

## Goal
Comprehensive unit + integration coverage for attestation/SBOM verification and policy gating.

## Unit Tests
- Valid attestation/SBOM passes.
- Missing or invalid signature fails with correct error code.
- SBOM hash mismatch fails.
- Attestation predicate digest mismatch fails.
- Policy overrides (allow_unsigned, min_slsa_level) behave as configured.

## Integration Tests
- Host install gating with test connector bundles:
  - Valid bundle installs.
  - Invalid bundle rejected.
  - Policy override allows dev-only bundle.

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON logs for each verification step (stage, input digest, result, duration_ms).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover success/failure paths with deterministic outputs.
- Logs are stable and redact sensitive fields.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

Adding comprehensive fcp-core/src/supply_chain.rs unit tests for all validation paths, deterministic hashing, signing bytes, and helper functions.","created_at":"2026-03-01T21:05:56Z"},{"id":312,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3gzz","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Completed comprehensive unit test suite for fcp-core/src/supply_chain.rs. Added 86 new tests (93 total, up from 7). Coverage includes:
- Attestation validation: wrong format, schema version, empty fields, invalid digests (prefix/algo/length/case), empty materials, bad material URIs/digests, metadata time ordering, invocation_id, slsa_level boundaries (0-5), builder_allowlist rules, trust root types (sigstore/tuf/manual/invalid), signature algorithm/key_id/signed_fields
- SBOM validation: wrong format, schema version, empty bom_version/tool_chain/components, component fields (id/name/version/hashes/licenses), unsupported hash algos, duplicate component_ids, dependency graph (empty/unknown refs, duplicates, empty targets), trust root and signature fields
- Deterministic encoding: CBOR/JSON canonical bytes, content hashing (blake3-256/sha256), signing bytes with domain separation, cross-encoding hash difference
- Serde round-trips: attestation/sbom JSON round-trip, predicate type URL serialization, SPDX format
- Helper functions: is_lower_hex_64 (empty/short/long/uppercase/non-hex), digest_is_supported (blake3/sha256/unknown/no-colon), hash_bytes output format, HashAlgorithm::as_str/default
- Cross-cutting: attestation vs SBOM signing bytes use different domains, error Display messages for all 5 variants
Quality gates: cargo test (93/93 pass), cargo clippy -D warnings (clean), cargo fmt --check (clean).","created_at":"2026-03-01T21:18:25Z"},{"id":993,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3gzz","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Added 18 integration tests in fcp-host/tests/supply_chain_integration.rs covering: happy path, deny missing attestation, deny missing SBOM, SLSA level gating, untrusted builder, digest mismatch, dev override, cache integration (serve/isolate/clear), deterministic evidence, audit events (cache hit/miss), JSON serialization round-trip, and SLSA boundary policy matrix. All 18 tests pass. Combined with the 86 core unit tests, this bead's acceptance criteria are fully met.","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:21:29Z"},{"id":1015,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3gzz","author":"GoldOx","text":"Re-verified the supply-chain test bead against the current fcp-host worktree.

Fresh verification in this session:
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.target-goldox-host-cache cargo test -p fcp-host --all-targets
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.target-goldox-host-cache cargo clippy -p fcp-host --all-targets -- -D warnings

Observed coverage on disk:
- host-side verification unit coverage in crates/fcp-host/src/supply_chain.rs
- 18 integration tests in crates/fcp-host/tests/supply_chain_integration.rs covering allow/deny paths, policy overrides, cache behavior, deterministic evidence, audit-event cache-hit behavior, and serialization round-trips

Important scope note: I am closing only this test bead. The broader host verification pipeline bead remains open because the current code search shows SupplyChainGate exists as a host module with tests, but it is not yet wired into a broader install/upgrade flow.
","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:27:03Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3iac","title":"Capability Usage Analytics + Least-Privilege Advisor","description":"# Capability Usage Analytics + Least-Privilege Advisor

## Goal
Measure real capability usage and suggest minimal capability sets per zone/connector.

## Why
- Reduces over-provisioned capabilities.
- Enables least-privilege hardening over time.
- Provides actionable operator insights.

## Scope
- Usage telemetry collection and aggregation.
- Analyzer to recommend minimal capability sets.
- CLI reports + export for automation.

## Deliverables
- CapabilityUsage event schema and aggregation store.
- Recommendation engine with explainable rules.
- CLI reports (usage, unused, suggested reductions).
- Unit/integration tests + E2E script with structured logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reports list unused capabilities and suggested reductions.
- Suggestions are deterministic, explainable, and redact sensitive data.","notes":"Added tests in crates/fcp-core/src/telemetry.rs to assert CapabilityUsageEvent sets format/schema_version and serde defaults when missing. Re-ran cargo fmt/check/clippy (all-targets).

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

Closing parent epic.","created_at":"2026-03-08T23:21:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3ijc","title":"fcp.redis: Redis Cache and Pub/Sub Connector","description":"# fcp.redis - Redis Cache and Pub/Sub Connector


## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant Redis connector for cache/storage, pub/sub, and stream operations with strict capability gating and bounded resource use.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints` (defined in the connector’s manifest/cap map bead if present).
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.
- For DB/queue connectors, constrain raw TCP connects to explicit host:port only; deny private ranges unless explicitly allowed by policy.

## Overview
Redis connector for caching, key-value storage, pub/sub messaging, and data structure operations.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage`
- `operational`
- `bidirectional`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Request/Response + Queue/Pub-Sub

## Key Operations

### String Operations
- `redis.get` - Get value
- `redis.set` - Set value (with TTL, NX/XX)
- `redis.mget/mset` - Multiple get/set
- `redis.incr/decr` - Atomic increment/decrement
- `redis.append` - Append to string
- `redis.getrange/setrange` - Substring operations

### Hash Operations
- `redis.hget/hset` - Single field get/set
- `redis.hmget/hmset` - Multiple fields
- `redis.hgetall` - Get all fields
- `redis.hdel` - Delete fields
- `redis.hincrby` - Increment field

### List Operations
- `redis.lpush/rpush` - Push to list
- `redis.lpop/rpop` - Pop from list
- `redis.lrange` - Get range
- `redis.llen` - List length
- `redis.lindex` - Get by index
- `redis.blpop/brpop` - Blocking pop

### Set Operations
- `redis.sadd/srem` - Add/remove members
- `redis.smembers` - Get all members
- `redis.sismember` - Check membership
- `redis.sinter/sunion/sdiff` - Set operations

### Sorted Set Operations
- `redis.zadd/zrem` - Add/remove members
- `redis.zrange/zrevrange` - Get by rank
- `redis.zscore` - Get score
- `redis.zrangebyscore` - Get by score range
- `redis.zincrby` - Increment score

### Pub/Sub Operations
- `redis.publish` - Publish message
- `redis.subscribe` - Subscribe to channels
- `redis.psubscribe` - Pattern subscribe
- `redis.unsubscribe` - Unsubscribe

### Stream Operations
- `redis.xadd` - Add to stream
- `redis.xread` - Read from stream
- `redis.xrange` - Read range
- `redis.xgroup` - Consumer groups
- `redis.xreadgroup` - Group read

### Key Operations
- `redis.del` - Delete keys
- `redis.exists` - Check existence
- `redis.expire/ttl` - TTL management
- `redis.keys/scan` - Key enumeration
- `redis.type` - Get key type

### Transaction Operations
- `redis.multi/exec` - Transactions
- `redis.watch` - Optimistic locking

## Providers Supported
- Redis OSS
- Redis Enterprise
- AWS ElastiCache
- Azure Cache for Redis
- Upstash
- Redis Cloud

## Connection Management
- Connection pooling
- Cluster mode support
- Sentinel support
- TLS connections

## Authentication
- Password authentication
- ACL users (Redis 6+)
- TLS client certificates

## Zone Architecture

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The *"zones"* described in this section are a **recommended deployment pattern** (run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies) and/or conceptual risk tiers.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

```
Zone 0 (Public):     N/A
Zone 1 (Authorized): Read operations
Zone 2 (Private):    Write operations
Zone 3 (Sensitive):  Admin operations, credentials
```

## Dependencies
- fcp-core, fcp-ratelimit, fcp-testkit, fcp-telemetry

## Acceptance Criteria
- The functionality described in this bead is implemented with typed schemas + validation (no unstructured “stringly” interfaces).
- Errors map to the FCP error taxonomy with correct retry semantics and no secret/PII leakage.
- Mock-only unit/integration tests cover success paths and common failures and emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T17:37:08.984070993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T23:03:59.782048Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T23:03:59.782035Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3ijc","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-iv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3ijc","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-krt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3ijc","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3ijc","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-zx0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1033,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3ijc","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Redis connector implemented: 14 operations (get/set/del/exists/expire/ttl/incr/hget/hset/hgetall/lpush/lrange/sadd/smembers), Upstash-compatible HTTP REST API, Bearer auth, full introspection/simulate/health/doctor, 169 tests all passing, clippy clean. Committed as e212a7a6. — SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-03-07T21:50:14Z"},{"id":1035,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3ijc","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Also see Redis commit e212a7a6 — 81 connectors now in workspace.","created_at":"2026-03-07T22:08:19Z"},{"id":1064,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3ijc","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Redis connector already built (connectors/redis, 14 ops, 169 tests, E2E compliance passing).  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3ioh","title":"[POL] E2E: policy bundle apply + rollback","description":"# [POL] E2E: policy bundle apply + rollback

## Goal
End-to-end script validating preview, apply, and rollback workflow.

## Script
- `e2e/policy_bundle_flow.sh` with structured JSON logs.
- Steps:
  1) Create bundle from baseline.
  2) Diff + risk summary.
  3) Preview with sample requests.
  4) Apply bundle and verify allow/deny changes.
  5) Roll back to prior bundle and verify restoration.

## Logging
- Emit JSON per step: {stage, bundle_id, result, reason_codes, duration_ms}.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rollback restores prior behavior with verified receipt.
- Logs show deterministic diff/preview outputs.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

Initial mapping: existing policy end-to-end coverage lives in crates/fcp-cli/tests/policy_e2e_test.rs and the repo has scripts/e2e/, but there is no top-level e2e/ directory at the moment. Reserved crates/fcp-cli/tests/policy_e2e_test.rs and e2e/policy_bundle_flow.sh while reconciling whether this bead should extend the existing Rust E2E harness, add a new scripts/e2e shell flow, or both.","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:23:08Z"},{"id":1017,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3ioh","author":"LilacWolf","text":"Implemented the policy bundle E2E flow in crates/fcp-cli/tests/policy_e2e_test.rs and added the repo-convention wrapper at scripts/e2e/policy_bundle_flow.sh, plus a scenario_registry.json entry. The bead text mentioned e2e/policy_bundle_flow.sh, but this repo’s existing registry and scripts live under scripts/e2e/, so I normalized to that path instead of inventing a new top-level e2e directory. Verification: remote rch cargo test -p fcp-cli --test policy_e2e_test e2e_policy_bundle_apply_and_rollback_flow -- --nocapture passed; bash scripts/e2e/policy_bundle_flow.sh passed when invoked with a remote CARGO_CMD; remote rch cargo check -p fcp-cli --all-targets passed. Residual warning remains unrelated in crates/fcp-sandbox/src/linux.rs:726 (unused_mut).","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:49:16Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-x9af.2","title":"fcp.jira: Provisioning Automation (Cloud OAuth / Server PAT)","description":"# fcp.jira: Provisioning Automation (Cloud OAuth / Server PAT)

## Goal
Automate Jira onboarding while keeping secrets zone-scoped.

## Requirements
- Cloud:
  - OAuth2 via AutomationRecipe (preferred)
  - API token only if policy allows
- Server/DC:
  - PAT/basic auth only if policy allows
- All secrets via `CredentialId`.
- Validate permissions and NetworkConstraints.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `fcp doctor` validates readiness.
- Fully mockable.

Added JiraAuth enum (Token{domain,email,api_token}/CredentialId{domain,credential_id}) with Clone, custom Debug (redacted), redacted_label, is_secretless, domain(). JiraConfig with strict xor validation (domain always required, then email+api_token xor credential_id). handle_doctor with 6 checks, handle_self_check with health_check via /myself. Updated handle_configure/handle_health. 64 tests pass (28 unit + 36 integration), 0 clippy warnings.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:59:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-x9af.3","title":"TEST: Jira Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"# TEST: Jira Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)


## Goal
Validate Jira connector via mock-only tests (issue/project ops, pagination, auth) with deterministic behavior.

## Must cover
- Manifest determinism + interface_hash.
- Cloud vs Server/DC API differences.
- JQL builder and pagination.
- ADF ↔ Markdown conversion.
- Attachment URL validation and bounds.
- Rate limits (429) and Retry-After.
- Redaction (no tokens, no raw issue bodies by default).

## Logging
Structured JSON logs with correlation IDs; include request/response metadata (redacted) and latency.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic in CI.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-x9af.4","title":"E2E: Jira Connector Compliance Run","description":"# E2E: Jira Connector Compliance Run

## Goal
Add Jira scenarios to the shared connector compliance runner.

## Scenarios
- Default deny.
- Allow with valid token.
- Network guard allow/deny (host-bound).
- Dangerous op gating (delete issue, admin actions).

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test --package fcp-e2e --features jira` passes deterministically.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-xme","title":"fcp.terraform: Plan generation and review (read-only)","description":"# fcp.terraform: Plan generation and review (read-only) (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe, read-only plan generation so agents can:
- run `terraform plan` deterministically
- parse plan output into a canonical object
- review diffs without making changes

## Scope
- Validate configuration (no mutations).
- Generate a plan artifact with:
  - stable hash
  - explicit backend/workspace context
- Render plan summary (adds/changes/destroys counts).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.plan` (read-only)
- `terraform.state.read` (may be required)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile must forbid:
  - interactive prompts
  - uncontrolled filesystem writes
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless backend/providers require explicit egress.
- Plan artifacts must be integrity-checked (hash) before any apply.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `terraform.validate`
  - `terraform.plan.generate`
  - `terraform.plan.show`
- Canonical plan object type:
  - resource change set
  - outputs diff
  - provider/version metadata

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with a terraform shim/fake fixture:
  - command args are correct (`-input=false`)
  - parsing correctness
  - bounds on stdout/stderr capture

## Acceptance criteria
- Plan generation is deterministic and safe-by-default.
